surface.name: App Platform Getting Started, with App Platform Quickstart as the general route and Deploy Sample Apps as the fully reconstructable candidate
surface.selection_basis: The official App Platform documentation places Getting Started first and describes App Platform Quickstart as getting source code live in a few minutes. The Quickstart uses the developer's repository or container image. The same Getting Started section links Deploy Sample Apps, whose control-panel route supplies a vendor-maintained starter and an observable Hello World style website terminal. The sample route is retained as a complete candidate without replacing the vendor's general Quickstart.

alternatives_considered.name: App Platform API or doctl automation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general Quickstart starts with the Control Panel and presents repository or image selection. The automation route additionally requires doctl or API credentials and an app spec.

alternatives_considered.name: Droplets
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Droplets are a separate infrastructure product, while the frozen category and official App Platform Getting Started surface provide a direct application deployment journey.

alternatives_considered.name: Functions
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Functions are a separate product surface and are not selected by the App Platform Getting Started index.

entry_point.developer_intent: Deploy a first application to DigitalOcean App Platform and open its public URL.
assumed_prior_state: The developer can use a web browser and the DigitalOcean Control Panel.
A DigitalOcean personal account with Control Panel access exists, or the developer can complete the separate signup gate.
For the general Quickstart, the developer has source code in GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket, or a container image in a supported registry.
For the fully reconstructed sample candidate, the developer can deploy directly from a DigitalOcean sample repository without creating a GitHub repository.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: A deployed sample app's basic Hello World style website is viewable in the browser.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: App Platform has automatically deployed the selected starter application and exposed it at the URL shown on the app Overview page.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The deployment completes, a URL appears at the top of the app Overview page, and opening it displays the sample app's basic Hello World style website.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: A deployed sample app's basic Hello World style website is viewable in the browser.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official Quickstart stops the Create an App path after deployment and directs the developer to view the app at the Overview URL. The sample-app guide states that each starter deploys a basic Hello World style website viewable after deployment. Scaling, databases, domains, customization, and redeployment are later work.

prerequisites.requirement: A DigitalOcean personal account with Control Panel access. The public App Platform quickstart does not document the atomic signup sequence.
The account may require a payment method for identity verification. App Platform has a free tier only for qualifying static-site apps; service components are paid according to selected resources.
If the GitHub repository route is used, the developer must have the Owner or Maintainer permission role for the repository and may need to grant DigitalOcean read access.
For the sample candidate, access to a starter in the Control Panel Templates tab. Deploying directly from the template repository avoids a GitHub account requirement.

primary_path.action: Open the official App Platform Getting Started page.
primary_path.output: The page presents App Platform Quickstart first and also links the sample-app deployment guide.
primary_path.success_signal: The App Platform Quickstart and Deploy Sample Apps routes are visible.

primary_path.action: Open Deploy Sample Apps to follow a fully specified starter path.
primary_path.output: The control-panel sample deployment instructions are available.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide identifies starter apps that deploy a basic Hello World style website.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The general Quickstart instead requires the developer to supply a repository or container image.

primary_path.action: Open the DigitalOcean Control Panel and authenticate to an existing account.
primary_path.output: The authenticated Control Panel is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A new developer must pass signup, possible verification, and possible payment-method gates. The public quickstart does not document the atomic signup screens, so that precondition remains unresolved.

primary_path.action: Click Create, then select App Platform.
primary_path.output: The Create an app screen opens.

primary_path.action: Select the Templates tab and choose or search for a starter app.
primary_path.input: A starter app from the template grid.
primary_path.output: The selected starter app is ready for deployment setup.
primary_path.success_signal: The starter app presents a Deploy action.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Starter availability may vary; the guide does not promise a particular language template.

primary_path.action: Click Deploy and choose Deploy from template repository.
primary_path.output: App Platform uses the sample app repository directly.
primary_path.success_signal: The creation flow proceeds without requesting repository ownership information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The peer Create new repository in GitHub branch requires selecting an owner, naming the repository, creating it, returning to DigitalOcean, refreshing the page, selecting GitHub and the new repository, and clicking Next.

primary_path.action: Detect the starter's app resources and prefill resource settings.
primary_path.input: The selected sample repository and its app spec and language-specific files.
primary_path.output: A Resource settings table is populated.
primary_path.success_signal: Detected component name, resource type, branch, build and run configuration, and related settings are available for review.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The developer can edit detected fields. The exact fields shown depend on resource type.

primary_path.action: Review the Resource settings table and edit any settings required by the selected starter, then continue.
primary_path.input: Detected settings, including component name, resource type, branch, instance size, commands, ports, routes, and environment variables where applicable.
primary_path.output: Resource settings are accepted for the app.
primary_path.success_signal: The flow advances beyond resource configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Web services must bind their HTTP port on 0.0.0.0 rather than localhost. Build or runtime requirements can vary by starter.

primary_path.action: Optionally add app-level environment variables and encrypt sensitive values.
primary_path.input: Any variables required by the chosen starter.
primary_path.output: Optional variables are attached to the app configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The flow can continue with or without optional variables when the starter has none.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A starter that requires external credentials can add undocumented, starter-specific prerequisites.

primary_path.action: If the Datacenter region selector is available, choose the region for the app.
primary_path.input: The regions offered in the Datacenter region dropdown.
primary_path.output: The selected deployment region is attached to the app configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The chosen region is displayed in the Datacenter region section.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Region selection is not available for static sites because static resources use DigitalOcean's global CDN.

primary_path.action: Review the app name and project, and create a new project only if desired.
primary_path.input: A unique app name and an existing project, or a new project name, purpose, and environment.
primary_path.output: The app is assigned to a project with a unique name.
primary_path.success_signal: The Finalize section accepts the selected app and project information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Creating a new project adds the name, purpose, and environment fields.

primary_path.action: Review the configuration and pricing summary.
primary_path.input: The detected or selected resource sizes and app settings.
primary_path.output: The developer can see the resources and applicable price before creation.
primary_path.success_signal: The Create App action is available after review.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Only qualifying static-site apps use the free tier. Other component sizes are billable, and account verification may require a payment method.

primary_path.input: The confirmed starter, resources, app information, and pricing.
primary_path.output: App Platform creates the app and starts deployment automatically.
primary_path.success_signal: The new app Overview and deployment state become available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This action provisions resources and may begin billable usage. It was documented but not exercised during research.

primary_path.action: Build and deploy the selected starter application.
primary_path.input: The sample repository and accepted app configuration.
primary_path.output: A deployed application and its DigitalOcean-provided public URL.
primary_path.success_signal: Deployment completes and the URL is shown at the top of the Overview page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Builds can take time and can fail for starter-specific build or runtime reasons. The general build timeout is one hour, but the quickstart does not state a typical duration.

primary_path.action: Open the URL at the top of the app Overview page.
primary_path.output: The starter application loads in the browser.
primary_path.success_signal: A basic Hello World style website from the selected sample is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A successful deployment does not guarantee application behavior for every possible template. This terminal is supported specifically for the sample apps described by the official guide.

candidate_paths.name: Deploy a DigitalOcean sample directly from its template repository
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This vendor-documented sample branch supplies the source and explicitly says no additional information is required at repository selection, while retaining the full control-panel configuration, creation, deployment, and browser-verification journey.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates the source-choice portion of the general App Platform Quickstart without silently choosing the developer's own language, repository, or image.

branches.condition: The developer does not yet have a DigitalOcean account
branches.path: Use the external signup flow. Official support pages establish that email, Google, and GitHub login methods exist and that verification, account lock, and payment-method checks can occur, but the public App Platform docs do not reconstruct their current order.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an unresolved account-creation and possible verification or billing sequence before the Control Panel path.

branches.condition: The developer uses the general Quickstart rather than a sample template
branches.path: Select GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, DigitalOcean Container Registry, Docker Hub, or GitHub Container Registry; choose the repository and branch or tag, optionally a monorepo directory and Autodeploy, then proceed to resource configuration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses developer-supplied code or an image and can add repository authorization, language detection, Dockerfile, and app-specific configuration requirements.

branches.condition: The developer wants a customizable copy of the starter
branches.path: Choose Create new repository in GitHub, select an owner, enter a repository name, click Create repository, return to Create an app, refresh, select GitHub and the new repository, then click Next.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds GitHub ownership and repository-creation steps before converging on resource configuration.

branches.condition: The selected starter needs environment variables or external credentials
branches.path: Edit app-level or resource-level variables, add the required keys and values, select their scope, and encrypt sensitive values before continuing.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds starter-specific credential and configuration gates that the generic guide cannot enumerate.

branches.condition: The selected starter is a static site
branches.path: Skip manual region selection because static resources are served through DigitalOcean's global CDN.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the region-selection action before Finalize without changing the deployment and browser-verification terminal.

branches.condition: The app contains only qualifying static-site components and the team is within free-tier limits
branches.path: Use the static-site free tier. Otherwise select a paid component size and accept usage-based billing.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Determines whether the first deployment is free or billable.

friction_gates.description: The documented app flow requires the authenticated Control Panel, but current public App Platform docs do not enumerate the signup screens.

Signup or login can trigger verification or account lock handling. The official support surface directs a locked signup to support and discusses emailed verification codes, but does not locate those states in the App Platform path.
DigitalOcean may require a payment method to verify identity. The exact eligibility and position of this gate are not stated by the App Platform quickstart.
The sample flow branches between creating a GitHub repository and deploying directly from the template repository.
Detected resource settings depend on the starter and resource type, and a web service must bind its HTTP port to 0.0.0.0.
The developer must review pricing. Only qualifying static sites have a free tier; other service resources are billed by selected size and usage.
App Platform builds and deploys before the public URL can be verified. The quickstart does not document a typical duration.

time_to_first_success.value: The App Platform Getting Started index says the Quickstart gets source code live in a few minutes; no exact duration is stated for the sample candidate.

sources.title: App Platform Quickstart
evidence_supported: Resource, environment, info and review sequence

sources.title: Deploy Sample Apps to App Platform
sections_used: Deploy a Sample App Using the Control Panel

sources.title: How to Create Apps in App Platform
sections_used: Create an App Using the Control Panel

sources.title: Logging In
sections_used: What do I do if my account was locked during sign-up?
How can I disable device verification?
Why didn't I receive a password reset email or verification code?
evidence_supported: Email, password, Google and GitHub authentication alternatives

sources.title: Why do I need to enter a payment method?
sections_used: Why do I need to enter a payment method?

sources.title: App Platform Limits
evidence_supported: Build wait and one-hour build timeout

uncertainties.question: What is the complete current signup, verification, and first-team sequence for a new DigitalOcean developer?
uncertainties.impact: The number, order, and possible waiting time of steps before the Control Panel cannot be reconstructed from the public App Platform quickstart.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public official pages establish that email, Google, GitHub, verification-code, account-lock, and payment-method states exist, but they do not present one chronological signup route.

uncertainties.question: Which exact starter template is selected for the shortest sample journey, and does it use only free static-site resources?
uncertainties.impact: Resource settings, environment requirements, price, build behavior, and rendered content vary by starter.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official guide presents a template grid and language list but does not name one canonical default starter.

uncertainties.question: Does every new account need to provide a payment method before a first App Platform deployment?
uncertainties.impact: A billing and identity-verification gate may appear before resource creation.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The support page explains why payment information is requested, but the App Platform docs do not state account eligibility rules or the gate's position.

excluded_after_success: Customizing the sample and pushing a change for automatic redeployment.
Scaling the app horizontally or vertically.
Registering a custom domain and configuring DNS.
Adding further components, jobs, workers, or functions.
Production hardening, monitoring, backups, and teardown.
